assertions.title: First Visit Flow

steps.observed: Intro skipped in headless mode (WebGL unavailable, expected per user-testing.md). Landing page displayed immediately.

steps.expected: Landing page with header, sections, CTAs
steps.observed: Landing page visible with WZRD.tech header, video background, card-based sections, Demo/Dashboard/Log Out buttons (auth bypass active).

steps.expected: /login page loads or redirects to /home (auth bypass)
steps.observed: Auth bypass auto-redirected from /login to /home (dashboard). URL confirmed as http://localhost:8080/home.

steps.expected: Dashboard page with project list, create button
steps.observed: Dashboard loaded with 0 projects, sidebar with All Projects/Kanvas, stat cards, Create your first project CTA, 0 credits.

evidence.consoleErrors: none on landing page; 2x 'controls.start() should only be called after a component has mounted' warnings on /home (Framer Motion, non-blocking)

assertions.issues: Auth bypass causes /login to redirect directly to /home, so the actual login form is not testable. The 'Log In' CTA is not visible on landing because auth bypass makes user appear authenticated (shows 'Dashboard'/'Log Out' instead). Flow still validates landing → login → dashboard path.

assertions.title: Color Consistency Across All Pages

steps.observed: Landing page uses hardcoded orange accents (#FF6B4A, orange-500, amber-500). Demo/Sign Up buttons use orange gradient. Section dividers use orange-500/20. Consistent orange theme.

Dashboard uses CSS variable --primary (HSL 168 76% 42% = teal/green) for key elements: 'Create Project' button, '+' icon, and primary action styling. Sidebar 'All Projects' highlight and 'New Pro...' button use orange. Mixed color scheme.
index.css --primary: 168 76% 42% (teal/green in dark mode), 168 76% 47% (teal/green in light mode). NOT orange/amber.

steps.expected: Only online indicators and success checkmarks use green
steps.observed: Home page 'Create Project' button and '+' icon use teal/green via --primary CSS variable. These are NOT exempt usage patterns (not online indicators or success checkmarks).

assertions.issues: CSS custom property --primary in index.css is still set to HSL 168 76% 42% (teal/green), not migrated to orange/amber. The landing page uses hardcoded orange (#FF6B4A, orange-500/amber-500 Tailwind classes) but the Home page and other pages using the --primary CSS variable show teal/green accents. This creates a visual inconsistency between the landing page (orange) and dashboard (teal/green for primary actions like 'Create Project').

assertions.title: Loading States Across Navigation

steps.observed: LoadingScreen.tsx line 126: src='/lovable-uploads/wzrdtechlogo.png'. Component renders logo centered with orange/amber animations (glow, rings, particles, progress bar).

steps.action: Verify LoadingScreen is used in App.tsx
steps.observed: App.tsx line 89: <LoadingScreen isLoading={isLoading} message='Initializing MOG Studio...' />. isLoading state starts true, set to false after 2s timer (line 73-77).

steps.expected: PerfShell used as fallback for lazy pages
steps.observed: App.tsx line 11: import PerfShell; line 69: const fallback = usePerfShell ? <PerfShell headline='Preparing studio' /> : null; line 94: <Suspense fallback={fallback}>. PerfShell wraps all lazy-loaded route components.
LoadingScreen.test.tsx line 26-29: tests that logo src is '/lovable-uploads/wzrdtechlogo.png'.

sourceCodeFiles: src/App.tsx (line 89: LoadingScreen, line 69+94: PerfShell Suspense fallback)

assertions.issues: LoadingScreen only displays for ~2s on initial load and is difficult to capture visually in automated headless testing. Source code inspection confirms correct implementation. No visual screenshot of loading screen captured (dismissed before browser automation could screenshot), but source code and unit tests verify the behavior.

steps.observed: File exists: /public/bgvid.mp4 (1,872,572 bytes, dated Mar 13 16:51).

steps.expected: File does NOT exist at repo root
steps.observed: ls: /Users/gratitud3/Downloads/mogstudio-repo/bgvid.mp4: No such file or directory. Confirmed not at repo root.

steps.action: Inspect video element src in browser DOM
steps.observed: DOM inspection: video src='http://localhost:8080/bgvid.mp4', currentSrc='http://localhost:8080/bgvid.mp4', autoplay=true, loop=true, muted=true.
VideoBackground.tsx line 59: src='/bgvid.mp4'. Correct Vite public path reference.

evidence.network: GET /bgvid.mp4 loaded successfully (video plays on landing page)

steps.observed: Landing.tsx line 8: const CinematicIntro = lazy(() => import('@/components/landing/CinematicIntro')); Comment: 'Lazy-load CinematicIntro so Three.js stays out of the initial bundle'.

CinematicIntro.tsx imports: '@react-three/fiber' (line 3), '@react-three/drei' (line 4), '@react-three/postprocessing' (line 5), 'three' (line 6). All imports are inside the dynamically imported module.
Landing.tsx: <Suspense fallback={<div className='fixed inset-0 z-[99999] bg-black' />}><CinematicIntro onComplete={handleIntroComplete} /></Suspense>. Wrapped in AnimatePresence + Suspense.

steps.action: Verify Three.js NOT in initial bundle imports
steps.expected: No top-level Three.js imports in Landing.tsx or App.tsx
steps.observed: Landing.tsx only imports React, react-router-dom, framer-motion, and other landing components. No three/react-three imports at top level. App.tsx has no Three.js imports. Three.js is isolated to the CinematicIntro lazy chunk.

frictions.description: Auth bypass (VITE_BYPASS_AUTH_FOR_TESTS=true) makes user appear pre-authenticated on landing page, showing 'Dashboard'/'Log Out' instead of 'Log In'/'Sign Up'. Cannot test the actual Login CTA click from landing page because it's not rendered.
frictions.resolution: Navigated directly to /login URL to test the login→dashboard redirect flow instead of clicking the Log In button on landing.

frictions.description: LoadingScreen appears for only ~2 seconds on initial app load and auto-dismisses. Cannot reliably capture screenshot of loading state in automated headless testing.
frictions.resolution: Verified LoadingScreen implementation via source code inspection and existing unit tests instead of visual capture.

frictions.description: WebGL/Three.js intro cannot render in headless Chromium (no WebGL support). Intro is auto-skipped, preventing visual verification of the intro animation.
frictions.resolution: This is documented as a known limitation in user-testing.md. Intro skip is correct fallback behavior.

summary: Tested 5 assertions: 4 passed, 1 failed. VAL-CROSS-001 (First Visit Flow): pass — landing page loads, navigating to /login redirects to /home dashboard via auth bypass. VAL-CROSS-005 (Color Consistency): FAIL — CSS --primary variable is still teal/green (HSL 168), not orange/amber. Landing page uses hardcoded orange (#FF6B4A) but Home page uses --primary for key actions (Create Project button, + icon), creating visible color inconsistency. VAL-CROSS-006 (Loading States): pass — source code confirms LoadingScreen uses wzrdtechlogo.png, PerfShell used as Suspense fallback. VAL-CROSS-012 (bgvid.mp4 Relocated): pass — file exists in /public, not at repo root, video src correctly references /bgvid.mp4. VAL-CROSS-013 (Three.js Lazy-Loaded): pass — CinematicIntro uses React.lazy() with dynamic import, Three.js packages isolated to lazy-loaded chunk.
